assertion_results.text: outputs/claude-md-result.md preserves the existing Security Configuration section with Product Lifecycle, Version Streams, and Source Repositories unchanged
assertion_results.evidence: claude-md-result.md lines 42-63 contain the full Security Configuration section with all three subsections. Product Lifecycle has all 5 fields matching the reference (Product pages URL: https://access.example.com/product-lifecycle, Jira version prefix: MYPRODUCT, Vulnerability issue type ID: 10200, Component label pattern: pscomponent:, VEX Justification custom field: customfield_12345). Version Streams table has the 2.1.x stream with identical values. Source Repositories table has both backend and frontend-ui with matching URLs. All values are identical to the reference file claude-md-configured-with-security.md lines 52-67.

assertion_results.text: outputs/discovery-log.md reports that Security Configuration is already fully configured or up to date
assertion_results.evidence: discovery-log.md Step 10 (lines 94-111) explicitly checks all Security Configuration subsections and concludes: 'Result: **Security Configuration is up to date.**' The summary (lines 131-136) lists Security Configuration among 'Sections already configured and up to date'.

assertion_results.text: outputs/changes-log.md indicates Security Configuration was preserved with no changes needed
assertion_results.evidence: changes-log.md line 5 states 'No changes were made to the Project Configuration. All existing sections are fully populated and up to date.' The section-by-section table (line 21) shows '## Security Configuration | No change | All subsections fully populated, no placeholder markers'. Files Modified section says 'None.'

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT offer the Security Configuration opt-in prompt — it detects the section already exists and is fully populated (idempotency skip)
assertion_results.evidence: discovery-log.md Step 10 (lines 94-111) detects the Security Configuration section exists, checks all subsections for placeholder markers, finds none, and reports 'Security Configuration is up to date.' There is no mention of an opt-in prompt being offered. The discovery log shows the skill went directly from detecting the section to reporting it as up to date, demonstrating idempotency skip behavior.

assertion_results.text: outputs/claude-md-result.md preserves all existing Repository Registry entries (backend and frontend-ui) unchanged
assertion_results.evidence: claude-md-result.md lines 5-8 show the Repository Registry table with both entries: 'backend | Rust backend service | serena_backend | /home/user/backend' and 'frontend-ui | TypeScript frontend | serena_ui | /home/user/frontend-ui'. These are identical to the reference file lines 14-17.
